review_text: Nice park. Steve the owner. Very friendly and helpful. Any maintainence issues were addressed in a timely manner. Great view of the jetty and sea views. Well priced. We would return

Nice caravan park. Steve the owner is super friendly and helpful. He is front of house and also undertakes maintainence issues. We had a couple of issue within the cabin. They were fixed vert quickly. The cabin we had was 9. A little dated but It was comfortable and well appointed. A tv in the main area and one in the main bedroom. Everything required was in the cabin. The cabin had a nice view of the jetty and sea views. Loved hearing the sound of waves crashing onto the beach. It was very quiet. Perhaps as it wasn’t school holidays.   We did the mangrove walk along the extensive board walk. The kiosk adjacent to the CP is very good also. Nice food friendly staff. Arno Bay is only a small town. But does have a hotel. A small supermarket and Post Office. We will return. 😎 Gary & Di

Didn’t stay But took a walk though to check it out.
Looks very untidy
Dirty amenities
Absolute filth in the camp kitchen.
Mice eaten pancakes on top of microwave Spoke to a lady and she said they had been there for 6 days.
Also commented on the ladies amenities saying they were dirty and some showers not working.
The grounds were a disgrace
So we stayed across the road behind the hotel $20 for 3 days and you get a $10 voucher to spend at the Pub, Cafe or Supermarket.

I will never stay here again, 2 bedroom @ a cost of $310 dollars for 2 nights. Friday/ Saturday
No one told us we couldn't use the tap water to fill the kettle, and they didn't supply a box of water. The place is filthy, mould on curtains, cobwebs everywhere. Saving grace we did have clean bedding.
Veranda and chairs covered in bird droppings, to the left of the cabin all along a dividing wall, piled high rubbish. Total breeding ground for rats and mice and anything else.
We arrived back at the cabin on the Saturday evening expecting to have a relaxing drink, to have a guy mowing the lawns. Then when it was dark, the chap started up the tractor, moving rubbish about.
This place is the worst cabin I have ever stayed in,  if it wasn't the fact,  we were going to a wedding on the Saturday, I would have left.

We have a pet friendly cabin booked for 10 days. We got there about 3.30 pm. the room had not been cleaned yet, so we had a walk around and oh my god, there was junk scattered everywhere. There was no porch for the dogs to run around in. The steps were not structural, held up with a plank of wood and some tiles. There was no railing to hold on to.  I sent 2 emails 2 weeks prior to going up there and got no reply. We turned around within a half an hour and returned to Adelaide.

A perfect place to base a stay as in the centre of so many other tourist towns.
To listen to the waves as so near the water was lovely.
The pancake breakfast on Sunday was a highlight.it was a very well equiped villa with all the kitchen supplies we needed.

When we arrived Steve was secretly filming us, which we found very strange. We got the key to chalet 11 it was quite dirty, but better than the car we were sleeping in. After we departed we found an extra $120 charge on our card. We challenged Steve about this and he claimed we had extra guests, which was untrue. He has since refused to refund. After asking around during our stay we learnt that he has a contract with the government to house people on tag, we heard some of these people have committed crimes against children. For a place that claims it is family friendly we find this totally unacceptable and morally wrong. Please think carefully before staying here. But don't let this horrible man take away from the beautiful town of Arno bay.
A great spot. Short walk to the jetty, Arno pub & the cafe.

Very expensive, what you pay for is not what you get.  Accommodation is dirty with mold.  No notification on the website that you can't drink their water as it's bore water.  Front office is not very welcoming and air conditioning doesn't work well.

We had 4 weeks here in our van great spot good fishing the wind at times can be a bit frustrating stev was a great host  and very hard working grounds were clean and tidy would recommend for a stop over
Helpful staff, good clean park.
Amazing place to stay, beach access and lots of shade. Shout out to Steve, what an amazing owner!!!
I stayed here for one night. Good water supply. Great location
Nice quiet little caravan park on the beach.Nice playground next door for the kids.Nice pub across the road and a jetty kiosk next door aswell
A very nice Caravan park, on sea front, clean amenities, friendly helpful staff 😀 close to hotel 🏨

Cracking location right on the beach with plenty of great walks and exploring around the area. Clean facilities, albeit a little dated but nonetheless a beautiful caravan park and huge shoutout to Steven who is extremely accomodating and helpful. Good quality country service with a smile and great chat!

The beachfront cabins are great, you can't beat the view of the jetty from the deck ! Fully self contained and fully equipped, there's everything you might need, 2 tvs and a dvd player. The rooms could have been a little cleaner but honestly it was fine. The owner was very friendly and welcoming, gave us a lot of local knowledge about the area and was very helpfull and available. Arno bay is a nice little town and the mangrove walk is must do !

We had two cabins and thankfully we did as we had to use equipment from each cabin to make one fully functioning kitchen. Oven did not work in one, the cabin where the ignition worked had a lighter and the one that did not have a lighter did not work. The place was not clean. You had to clean everything before you used it, and you were given a cut down square of scrubber to clean with. Not to mention the mould on all the curtains. Holes in flyscreens, letting in mozzies. The kids covered in mozzie bites. Shower curtain small allows water out of the shower and pools on the floor. The place lacks any maintenance.

Absolutely beautiful place to relax. We have stayed here many time and loved it ever time.
This time we stayed in the pet friendly cabin and it was fantastic. Cabin was very clean had all we needed Steve never hesitated to help if we needed anything.

Steve never stops always doing something in the park to keep the up keep of it .

The happy hour Saturday afternoon at 4pm was great meeting new people ,sunday pancake breakfast just bring a plate the rest is sorted the shed was very clean and tidy The public facilities (shower/toilet) were also very clean .
This is a beautiful family friendly caravan park its a must go stay/see  even if its a night or a weekend or even holidays.
The beach is but a stones throw away with a good fishing spots you have the jetty , large chess board and large checkers for the kids of all ages .
There is lots to see .

Location was great. Close to the walkway, boardwalk and able to walk into town. Unfortunately the amenities were very disappointing. Very unclean. Many toilets were out of order, showers were unclean, many cobwebs, dead flies ,spiders everywhere. Showers with missing rose. Toilets would not stop flushing. The site we were given was nice overlooking the bay but the taps constantly dripped and the power connection was dodgy.

We had a lovely time at the park. Took our dog Ellee and had a pet friendly cabin. The cabin was lovely and clean. Nothing was too much trouble for Steve the owner, he went out of his way to make sure we were comfortable. Right next to the beach is also a bonus.

Used SA Gov. Coast is Calling 5 day voucher. We have a dog so we could not use his beachfront villa which was the offer.
Steve, the owner / operator was very accommodating and allowed us to use his pet friendly house instead. While it was quite old it was clean and comfortable enough and everything worked fine. Steve is a lovely man who puts in a big effort and works hard but looks after the park basically on his own so it is not as pristine as some. He was very helpful with local knowledge of places to visit etc. Gave us a complimentary bottle of wine too, nice. Beach affected by algal bloom. We suffered itchy eyes etc. every time we walked the dog there so eventually stayed away. Everyone very friendly. Pub next door was good. Overall, a good trip.
This could be a top park but it doesn't look like anything gets done the lawns need mowing and the grounds need raking

Had a wonderful,  restful holiday. Excellent Villa's on the beachfront. Very comfortable queen size bed, amazing hot shower, fabulous place to stay. Recommend to all and will be back ourselves.  Thanks Steve
I stayed in a cabin and it was quite clean and comfortable. The beds are comfortable and the rooms are spacious

Steve and his wife Joanne are a lovely couple.
Sites are right on the beach front, also there are some cabins.
Enjoyed the pancakes and $3:50 coffee on Sunday mornings.
Just love the beach what a fantastic place to just walk relax and really enjoy the beauty 😊

I recently stayed here several nights for work.
Not too bad of a stay but overal I'd like to say: It's dated, cabins looked tired and worn.

Things that show this are: lights not working, kitchen utensils broken, barely space to move in bathroom, no hot water, toilet seat broken, no sink plug, beds rocksolid hard, lounge in cabin is backbreaking and painfull to sit on.

Location is great, being directly on the beach overlooking the bay from the balcony and if you are lucky to not have neighbours like I did you'll enjoy a relaxing day.

The cafe next door has decent coffee and produce and serves also as a small utility store with basic food supplies and fishing gear.

Serivce by the owner was top-notch. No request was too much nor ignored and everything I asked for was promptly addressed.

Difficult to sum up: For a night's stopover on a long drive definitely worth while ( good price vs quality) but anything more than a night's stopover I wouldn't recommend, definitely not with multiple people as you'll struggle for space inside the cabin.

Bring your own food and gear as not all cooking equipment working.

I found the cabin very clean and it has proper heating/cooling
Great location, needs a tidy up.

Staffs are very friendly and helpful. We stayed in a cabin with kitchen, 1 queen bed and a bunk bed. Everything is clean, the bed is comfy, they have hot water and aircon. The caravan park are also close to the jetty, the pub, cafe, and walkable to the mangrove boardwalk.
Great spot, great prices. Will visit again soon!

The location is wonderful with the jetty so close, a lovely boardwalk through the mangroves is brilliant, unfortunately we didn’t stay long enough to visit the pub to test the meals which is walking distance as well as a cafe,
Sadly our tired cabin didn’t match the lovely surroundings,
Staff were very friendly and when we had left an item behind Steve exhausted every option he could think of to get it back to us as quickly as possible,

Random pull in. Stayed onsite 8 which has a slab and is waterfront with direct access to the jetty and beach. It's walking distance to the mangrove boardwalk which is the best we've ever seen and a real credit to the community. We didn't use any services so can't comment. Yes, the grounds need a tidy up. Thanks for having us Steve. Arno Bay is a great little spot ❣️

My room cabin right on the front the beach
GREAT VIEWS
Don't go to this place, not one good thing to say about it, need a new cleaner for starters, the grounds look like Steptoe and sons live there, just don't go, don't!

The 3 bedroom house  is old, but clean. The washing machine doesn't work. There is a TV. (In the pamphlet, it says )they have cable TV system and free to air TV and satellite fee to air.  (We didn't use this. )
They have a happy hour and a free sausage sizzle. Sat at around 4 pm.
Free pancake breakfast Sunday 8.30am. $3.50 for Steve's famous coffee. ( I enjoyed this)
Afternoon tea Tuesday 2.30pm free coffee.
The toilets are clean. Yes they have a disabled toilet and shower. ( the toilet is a bit low)
The laundry is ok. 2 washing  machines, 1 dryer. They are old, but they work. You need to bring your own washing powder and pegs. There are 10 clothes lines. And a book exchange. (Love these)
Steve (the guy who runs the place) is nice and very chatty. He's always on the go, doing things.
Camp kitchen is old. Bring your own tea towel. ( I didn't use this)
A bit basic better in the pub car park for a short stay

Beautiful location with a very dismal and disgraceful caravan park. Steve the owner/ manager is nice enough but seriously has NO clue on cleanliness and tidiness. We were there for 11 nights and the ladies toilet & showers were not cleaned and neither was the mens according to my husband. There were broken toilets and showers that looks like they had been out of action for a very long time, spiders and webs everywhere, toilets sometimes flushed and there was a very strong smell of sewerage on some days, even before the Easter rush got there. In all seriousness surely under oh&s there has to be a point where health department needs to be contacted or at least council does an assessment to deem the park suitable to operate or be closed, which would be sad for the town should that happen. Honestly with some decent money spent and an operator that actually CARES about the park it could be a goldmine

Steve was great , he made room for us last minute over the Easter period. Affordable  and great location.  Night time u can hear the beach.  Amenities worked. Great Broadway close by and kids park across the road.  Free pancakes on Sunday.  Everyone was friendly.

Beautiful beach ..great jetty ..and it stops there .we certainly won't be back. Poor condition..dirty amenities which I did not see cleaned in the 5 days we stayed there .. kitchen area absolutely filthy ..very disappointed
A great caravan park with lots to do around

Great experience here! So well priced, finally. So hard to find these days. Powered site for a 7m RV, 2ppl for $35. Amenities are great, big kitchen space (no cooktop) but think we saw a BBQ, toilets, showers old but clean. And so close to the beach and jetty. Lots of lovely trees. Can watch nice sunset and sunrise. Super friendly and welcoming service
Too many biting sand flies  I was covered with bites. The jetty was nice.

Enjoyed our stay at this caravan park
Steve is a character, very friendly and chatty
The outlook we had to the jetty is one of the best
Fishing was quiet but the KG we caught were a decent size
Squiding off jetty loads of fun
We caught some but they weren’t plentiful
A busy bee is needed and some TLC
Maybe offer to prune roses or help out instead of site fee would work?
Look forward to returning to this wonderful part of South Australia next year
The Eyre Peninsula is worth the visit  (especially cos we’re from WA)
Place is dirty.
Tired, run down. Not worth the drive.
Just don’t. You have been warned

Tried several times to book - very difficult to get onto anyone. Finally got someone to answer & booked 6 months in advance. No confirmation provided. Got very nervous having read reviews ( we will be in motor bikes so need a clean comfortable bed at the end of the day) so cancelled (after several attempts again to make contact). Charged full booking amount with still 3 months before booking, & after yet another phone call were told they would be keeping a 20% admin fee! Think twice before booking here!

Beautiful location
Great kids playground
Great views
Super-friendly staff
Good fishing

Ok hard to review as we actually enjoyed our 3 days and we did get put on a nice caravan site BUT honestly it’s the most filthy, unkept dirty park we have stayed in. The camp kitchen is nothing short of DISGUSTINGwith no bins, filthy outdated facilities and rubbish everywhere, we would NOT use this camp kitchen under any circumstance. The park need a huge clean up as there is rubbish everywhere it looks like a tip. I did however find the ladies bathrooms very good and clean. Laundry was putrid aswell. Owner seemed to be a friendly guy but he needs to hire someone to help with running and cleaning up. Overpriced for the state of the park and lack of facilities

If I could give LESS than 1 star I would!!
Very unkept, junk everywhere, unhygienic and unsafe grounds. Disgusting service by manager Steve who overcharged us $5 each night (unpowered $28 with no astrix on website to say about peak season unlike powered site which we were charged at). He would not accept his mistake or refund. Water also had a silt like substance through it so not drinkable.
If in town stay out back of the pub! Great service and some money goes to charity!

Steve is fantastic
Right on the beach what more do you wish for

Arno Bay caravan park is in a fantastic location. The owner Steve is a great guy but struggles to keep up with all the maintenance and work required. He has Saturday afternoon happy hour and Sunday morning free pancakes with $3.50 coffees. We caravan so I cannot comment on the cabin accommodation but he has budget to deluxe available. We stay every year to enjoy the fishing and peaceful atmosphere.
We stayed in the beachfront villa's! Extremely clean cabin situated on a spectacular beach next to the jetty! Very affordable and great value. We will come again.
Very friendly and super helpful staff, with great cabins and an awesome view.

This caravan park is in a beautiful beachside location but is screaming for a lot of loving care. There is rubbish all over the place and equipment is old and not cared for. There seems to be very little maintenance done. Having said that it is quiet and peaceful with some good grassed sites

Great little caravan park, location perfect right on the beach next to the pier.  Bathrooms very clean, lots of toilets & showers. Creek up the road  was ideal for fishing out of our kayak. Pub next door very handy. Locals friendly and helpful.
nice, friendly guy and son.  Good amenities,  nice place

Excellent location , great walk along the mangrove boardwalk , great jetty at your doorstep , pub is right behind the caravan park
Steve the manager is lovely and very helpful, just a fantastic place to stay
Very untidy. Needs a lot of TLC.
Disgusting, unclean, unkempt Park. Laundry is appalling and machines don't work. Owner is never there. Such a shame as its a great town and lots to offer but the park is not a drawcard.

We were encouraged to stay at Arno Bay SA by a fella we met at Streaky Bay who had fond memories of family holidays when growing up. No doubt it seems this place has seen it's hay days and it is looking a little tired in spots. That doesn't matter, my initial impression of this place has changed and I'm now glad we came here.
The tourist park offers a number of positives, there is the pub right across the road, a cafe adjacent and for the fishing enthusiast the jetty is right there at the corner of the park and offers great fishing.
The beach is white sand contrasting beautifully with the turquoise water and it squeaks beneath your feet. Just adjacent to the park there is a board walk which takes you through the mangrove swamp and estuary which is just stunning and wheelchair accessible. It seems the locals love their little corner and have rolled sleeves up when required as there are various shelters , toilets and BBQ facilities provided by them along the way with the help of a few investors.
All in all, definitely worth a stop in for a few days.

Stayed here in our van two weeks ago.  Fabulous location, very quiet, friendly owner and at the time we stayed immaculately clean facilities.  Yes it's old and yes could be spruced up but beats the sterile 'group' parks hands down.

Over priced run down  put a caravan onsite for 12 mnths payed in advance so couldnt move the van till the 12 mnths were up in the time i was there we had no water as the tap to conect to was stuffed asked at least once a week for it to be fixed and wasnt and from what i hear still hasnt power is dodgy and boxes have not been upgraded since around 1970 we had our power box burn out as none of the fuses in the box worked when we asked for it and the meat in the freezer to be replaced we were told it was our fault because we left the power on  . The bloke who runs it  is a real sleaze as my partner will tell u .
Absolutely great place
Stayed behind pub all good had a feed in pub
We loved this caravan park. Had a home feel , Steve was accommodating and helpful. Will definitely come back for a longer stay next time. Thanks guys. See you soon

Stayed here recently. We asked for a site with no trees and were put under a big tree that dripped a salty , acidic liquid all over our van. We spoke to the owner who offered us another site, but it had trees as well. The whole place is a mess, toilets were in severe need of maintenance, cobwebs everywhere, toilet doors that don’t lock as well. The whole place needs a major make over. We left early because of the trees and the park was packed solid, was not enjoyable at all. Won’t be back.

Location lovely. Men's amenities are disgusting only 1 toilet usable all the others were blocked.
Checked in and given a site, but when we got there it was already taken. He told me that we could have a site with grass but we have to stay for 2nts. Also, it costs $2pn extra for grass as we only wanted 1nt and I'd paid for 1nt he chased me up in the afternoon for the extra $2.
Advice, Give this place a big miss.

The only thing this park has going for it is the location which is fantastic. |Bathrooms and camp kitchen are disgusting. Filthy.|No cleaning done during the entirety of my stay.|Speaking to other business owners in the town they expressed their concern and frustration about the state of the park.|If people stop staying there other businesses will suffer. |I don't think the park will get much return business. |I don't know how the Council allows the park to operate in this disgusting unhygienic state.

This little park is in a great position right on jetty. But facilities whilst quite good are not properly maintained. Both men's and women's toilets suffer with poor maintenance. Cobwebs on windows indicate very long time since attention. Grass outside is unkempt. Booking arrangements are lax. Yes wecenjoyed our stay here but it could be so much better.

Terrible park, dirty and small sites. Dirty amenities weren't cleaned the whole time we were there. Showers running continuously and couldn't be used, toilet doors don't lock. Thick spider webs and spiders everywhere, we had to leave early as couldn't bear it any more

So disappointed very overgrown  sites small. Staff friendly but the ablution blocks terrible, spider webs everywhere, doors don't lock,wasn't cleaned the whole time we were there. Nothing is dog friendly in the area office a shambles, very unkempt park would not return, left early. Close to beach but that is all it has going for it.
Best place ever don't drive past drop in you won't regret it

Relaxed laid back setting on the beach. Lots of shady trees. Steve / owner operator is super friendly, chatty & knowledgable. |Camp kitchen has huge tv lots of tables & chairs. Coffee & free pancakes on Sundays. The mangrove boardwalk is a good walk to do if not too hot or sunset would be fab. Lots of weed on bch now (March) but overall a relaxed stay. Dog cleaning bath enclosure too. Everything you need + sells nice red wine from award winning cellars in SA.
We stopped for a look beautiful beach ⛱️
Ideal spot. Friendly service. Great view.
Lovely! Be there Saturday for the sausage sizzle and beers. Nice people, nice spot.
Not bad pretty good
Good place to stay
Small very friendly park, right on the jetty

Beautiful small town caravan park. We got to watch the sun rise over the jetty. Steve was a great host! We enjoyed the Sunday pancakes and coffee 10/10. Not enough caravan parks like this these days, highly recommend.

We are from Melbourne and we loved this caravan park. Not the modernist, however people are friendly, Jetty is great for fishing. Country style Saturday bar night and lovely Sunday pancake morning. Please support small business so we still have this Beatiful for our country Australia.

Der Caravanplatz war sehr gut gebucht. Wir waren mit unserem Caravan dort. Der Platz ist relativ klein und eng. Der Platzwart ist sehr bemüht. Wir hatten noch nie so einen engagierten Platzwart. Es war immer was los. Happy Hour am Samstag, Sonntag Pancake-Frühstück und am 1. Januar den ganzen Tag Programm für alt und jung. Es war richtig viel los. Haben Sie Kinder?  Dann Neujahr in Arno Bay. Wir hatten eine Karte erhalten von einem richtig tollen Scinic Drive. Danke schon allein dafür. Allerdings gibt es einen Minuspunkt. Die Sauberkeit in den Duschen und Toiletten hätte besser sein können. Sorry aber grade wenn man so gut gebucht ist, sollte darauf sehr geachtet werden. 2 Waschmaschinen waren auch zu wenig. Vielleicht einfach mal kritisch hinterfragen, unser Tipp.

Great location, on the beachfront in Arno Bay, situated right in front of the jetty.
Cabins are older style, and fairly well stocked. Run on gas mains which is awesome. However, rooms are very dirty/unhygienic. Black mould on windows and on sink seals. All cupboards need a thorough cleaning and everywhere needs a decent dusting. Had to go buy disinfectant to feel comfortable staying. Such a shame as its an amazing place. Will be looking into private rentals for our next visit to Arno.
Very quiet and relaxing place
Beautiful old caravan park, needs a little attention, but its a lovely spot.  The mangrove walk is a must, the pub has great meals
Lovely grassed bays. Amenities need maintenance but reasonably clean. Close to jetty and beaches. Service is great and pancakes on Sunday. Overall a great stay
Park location is close to the jetty, the host friendly, however, the park is in desperate need of maintenance and refurbishment.
Good little Park, could be cleaned up as lawns needed mowing ,camp kitchen needs to be seriously cleaned

Great small park just near the jetty.  The park is kept neat and tidy, showers are hot, and amenities clean.  Steven is a great host and very knowledgeable with regard to fishing  in the area. Sausage sizzles on Saturday and pancakes for Sunday breakfast.  Cafe very close by and the pub across the road.  Small food market just down the road.
Always love this spot. Such a beautiful beach, white sand. Great Jetty for fishing. An older van park, clean but in need of refurbishment. Has Happy Hour on Saturday and pancake breakfast Sunday.

Host Steve is lovely. Location is right on beach front. Value for money. Camp kitchen facilities wurh a cosy fire seating.. Has some drive through sites. Toilets were clean/ showers hot, being done up. Dump point.   Tuesday afternoon was coffee get together.Sunday morning pancakes. Cafe on outside of park. Large play ground nearby. Jetty and close fishing spots.
Van Park beach front jetty right there nice park good hot showers happy hour
